title: Treat the LLM judge as a biased instrument
description: An LLM-as-judge is calibrated against human labels and its known biases are controlled before its scores are trusted.
body: Use when scoring outputs with another model as judge. Do not treat judge scores as ground truth, and avoid prompts that expose position, length, or self-preference biases. Actions: calibrate the judge against a human-labeled sample to measure agreement, randomize option order, control for length, and report judge-human agreement alongside scores. Validation signals include an agreement metric (e.g. correlation or kappa) between judge and human on a held-out sample. Risk boundary: a judge with low human agreement is an uncalibrated instrument; do not base a quality claim on it without human verification.
